name: Bing: Images Download
description: Run a Bing Images search in a fresh tab and download the thumbnails through Chrome's download manager into `~/Downloads/<download_folder>/`. Use when you need files locally and want them to flow through the authenticated browser (firewalls, geo-blocks). Do not use to get metadata or original high-res URLs (use `bing_images_search`, then wget the `high_res_url`s).

browser_automation.description: Navigates a fresh tab to /images/search, waits for the thumbnail grid, and streams matching `th.bing.com` thumbnails through Chrome's download_images action.

sequences.name: bing_images_download
sequences.description: Open Bing Images -> wait for grid -> download thumbnails.

required_variables.name: download_folder
required_variables.description: Subfolder under ~/Downloads to save files into

steps.name: Allow the image grid to hydrate

help.summary: Download up to 20 Bing Images thumbnails through Chrome into ~/Downloads/<folder>/.

parameters.name: download_folder
parameters.description: Subfolder under ~/Downloads to save the thumbnails into.

examples.command: rzn-browser run bing images-download --param search_query="cats" --param download_folder="cats"
examples.description: Saves up to 20 cat thumbnails to ~/Downloads/cats/.

help.returns: Chrome download manager writes files. Step result reports `downloaded` count.
notes: Only thumbnails (Bing-hosted under `th.bing.com`) — the host origin's high-res is not fetched here. For real high-res use `bing_images_search` + wget.
Chrome may prompt the first time you run against a fresh profile. Subsequent runs reuse the prior choice.
